name: Brand Clarity And Video Storytelling - Workshop
description:
First Brick Collective is bringing together two different but equally important parts of building something: shaping your idea and understanding how to bring it to life through video.
Sudhir Kiran will be walking participants through the basics of building a brand and finding the right words to explain what they’re building, and Saneki will introduce how to think about video: what to make, why it matters, and how to approach it with clarity and intent.
Two perspectives. One foundation. One powerful afternoon.
Limited seats. Link in bio.
Age: 18+

name: Divine Delicacies - Storytelling and Menu Tasting Session
description:
Food is such an integral part of our lives that we cannot imagine any worship without food. Keeping this in mind, we welcome you to a unique experience where we shall serve you samples of prasadams from various temples and share stories of places of worship and the perceived food preferences of the divine. Join Aparna Jaishankar and CA Sujata Shukla Rajan for this special collaboration, celebrating a taste of the divine.
Sujata has authored “Bhog Naivedya: Food Offerings To The Gods”, exploring food stories, mythology and the diverse food offerings to deities, at 56 ancient and renowned temples in India. Her forthcoming book, to be published by Rupa Publications is: “Festival Foods of India: A Celebration of Food, Heritage and Culture.”
performer: by Aparna Jaishankar and CA Sujata Shukla Rajan

name: No Is A Complete Sentence - A journaling workshop on boundaries
description:
For so many of us, saying “no” comes wrapped in guilt, over-explaining, and the quiet fear of disappointing others. This workshop is a gentle, honest space to unlearn that.
Through guided journaling, reflective prompts, and conversations, we’ll explore what boundaries really mean in our everyday lives, with friends, family, work, and most importantly, with ourselves. You don’t need to be “good at journaling” or have it all figured out. Just come as you are.

This is for women who are tired of stretching themselves too thin, who want to choose themselves without apology, and who are ready to practice saying no, clearly, calmly, and without guilt. All materials will be provided.
Age: 18+
performer: by Yukti & Women in the hood

name: THE COMMUNITY ART CIRCLE - Wonders Of Team Work In Art And Story Making
description:
A fun take on art with a community team spirit.
Seated in a circle layout, the imagination comes to life with basic art skills, inspiring stories and creative expressions.
Take home a creation inspiring stories, memories and a lot of interpretations!
Art. Story. Repeat!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.

name: Read A Kitaab - Book Club
description:
Some books don’t end when you finish them…they call you back. 
A line you missed.
A feeling that changed.
A story that reads differently the second time around.
This time at our Bangalore meetup, we’re diving into:
Debut Authors & Re-Reads
-the first books that impressed us,
and the ones we couldn’t help but return to.
Because sometimes, the second read hits harder than the first. 
Come with a book you’ve re-read (or want to!)
and let’s talk about why it stayed.
 
📍 Atta Galatta, Indiranagar, Bengaluru
📅 Sunday, 26th April
⏰ 5 PM – 7 PM
 
🎟️ Free entry | Registration mandatory
📞 +91 9082076420

name: Folktales, Myths and Legends from the Deccan - Book Launch
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Folktales, Myths and Legends from the Deccan, a captivating collection by award-winning author Nitin Kushalappa. Drawing from the rich oral storytelling traditions of the Deccan, this book gathers ten powerful narratives that travel across the plateau’s plains, forests, hills and coastal regions—lands where heroic ballads, possession rituals and sung epics have flourished for centuries.
From the tale of Junjappa, the herdsman-hero who rises to become a folk deity in the plains of Bayaluseeme, to the legend of warrior-sorcerer Kalyat Ponnappa in the misty mountains of Kodagu, and the epic of divine twins Koti and Chennaya from Tulunād, these stories reveal a world where heroes are worshipped, warriors are immortalized and goddesses walk among people. The collection also includes a haunting love ballad from Chhattisgarh, myths tracing the origins of communities in Kerala, and an ethnographic narrative on the revered tradition of the seven mother-goddesses. Meticulously researched and lyrically retold, Kushalappa’s work transforms oral histories into vivid stories that reflect the Deccan’s cultural diversity and enduring traditions.
Nitin Kushalappa is the author of ten books, including several on the history of Kodagu and the Kodava people. A recipient of the Sahitya Akademi Bal Sahitya Puraskar (2025) for Dakshin: South Indian Myths and Fables Retold, he brings deep historical insight and sensitivity to his storytelling. Join us for an engaging evening celebrating the living legacy of the Deccan’s myths, legends and folklore.
Age: 14+

name: A Summer of Creativity - 5 day Summer Art Camp
description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..
Let’s swap that for creativity 💫
Join Summer Art Camp for a fun-filled 5 days where kids explore different art mediums, express themselves, and take home their own creations 🎨
✨ Canvas painting
✨ Fluid art 
✨ Upcycled coconut shell art
✨ Hand-building with clay
✨Collage making ( Self-portrait )
 
⏰ 11 AM – 1 PM
📍 Atta Galatta
💰 ₹5000 (5 days) | ₹1200 (single day)
🎨 All materials included
Limited spots only!
Register now via the link in AG’s bio 💛
Age: 6-13 yrs

name: Memory Maps- A doorway to your radiant self - Workshop
description:
Memory Maps is a creative workshop where we journey through your memories using doodling, writing, and art—to create a visual map of your lived experiences that gently guides you to build an abundant self-image.
Theme: Gratitude & Self-Image
At the heart of this workshop is gently acknowledging your blessings—both known and forgotten—and discovering your self-image through powerful I AM affirmations.
Come as you are, and leave with a more radiant sense of self—opening space for expansive love, joy, magic, and aligned dreams.
 

🕰️ Date & Time

Date: 1st May 2026
Day: Friday (It's also Buddha Purnima and May day- Come ease into your weekend!)
Time: 5.30 - 7.30 PM
Duration: 2 hours
Arrival Note: Participants need to arrive 15 minutes early

 

 
📍 Venue

Venue Name: Atta Galatta
Location/Area: Basement floor
Seating Type: Floored Seating with carpet and cushions
Space Note: A shared space -creative circle with all materials provided 
Presentation included for guided prompts

 

🎟️ Ticket Details
Price: ₹1000/-
Includes:

A welcome note
Intuitive selection of an aligned message from an affirmation deck
A thoughtfully curated goodie pack!
A guided space for creative expression and memory map-making
A safe and supportive environment to reflect on your journey, immerse in gratitude, and strengthen your self-image
Meaningful connections with like-minded aligned participants
Access to a 21-day WhatsApp accountability group with Project Khwaab, guided by Vajjrashri Anand, to deepen and strengthen your self-image

 

🎨 What You’ll Experience


A safe and guided space to reflect on your journey and create your personal Memory Map


Reconnecting with moments of joy and gratitude from your unique lived experiences


A warm, intimate circle of shared presence and meaningful connection


Leaving with a tangible map of your story and a more grounded, radiant sense of self


Continued support through a 21 day WhatsApp group to deepen and fully embody your radiant self-image.


 

🧠 Who This Is For

Anyone seeking self-reflection and a deeper connection with themselves
For anyone who is inspired by creative expressions- mapping of your journey, reflecting and seeing your true light and brilliance through your own story
Creatives, thinkers, and dreamers who enjoy expression through art or writing
Anyone feeling called to pause, reflect, and reconnect within
No prior art or writing experience needed

 

🚫 This May Not Be For You If

If you’re not open to reflection or introspection in this season of life.

 

🎂 Age Group

18+

 

🧾 What to Bring

Just bring yourself, as you are! 
All materials are provided.

name: Boundary Commissions: Love Poems - Book Launch
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Boundary Commissions: Love Poems, the newest addition to the Westland Poetry Series by poet Kala Krishnan. This evocative collection traces the shifting terrains of love, exploring the invisible boundaries that lovers create and erase as time, circumstance and emotion reshape their relationship.
Drawing inspiration from the rich vocabulary of Sangam poetry, Indian classical music and cartography, Kala Krishnan weaves a lyrical narrative of two lovers whose journey becomes a meditation on language itself—its romance, elasticity and alchemical promise. The poems move fluidly between intimacy and distance, mapping emotional landscapes with sensitivity and depth.
Kala Krishnan writes, teaches and lives in Bangalore, where her work often blurs the boundaries between poetry and prose. Deeply fascinated by the possibilities of language, she brings to this collection a thoughtful and layered voice shaped by her love for literature, teaching, family and community. Join us for an intimate evening celebrating poetry, language and the many contours of love.

Age: 16+
performer: by Kala Krishnan in conversation with Nithin Manayath and Tirtha Pratim Das

name: When the Moon Burned White - Book Reading & Author Interaction
description:
Step into a deeply moving world where love refuses to surrender.
Join us for an intimate book reading session with Mohan K. Thangaraju as he brings alive When the Moon Burned White—a powerful story of love tested by loss, confinement, and injustice.
At a time when freedom is stripped away and hope feels distant, this story follows a man whose love stands unbroken—quietly enduring, fiercely loyal, and impossibly resilient. Through evocative storytelling and emotional depth, the novel explores how human connection becomes an act of courage in the face of adversity.
Blending intense emotional landscapes with gripping social realities, the book captures:
Love that defies boundaries
Strength in the face of injustice
The quiet power of hope and sacrifice
A deeply human, character-driven journey
More than just a reading, this session offers a rare opportunity to hear directly from the author—his inspiration, his process, and the emotional truths that shape his storytelling.
✨ For readers who believe that even when everything is taken away, love can still fight back.
Age: 20-50 yrs

name: Handmade Book Binding - A workshop
description:
In this hands-on workshop, participants will be introduced to the fundamentals of bookbinding. They will learn the basics of the craft, including key terminology, tools, and materials commonly used in traditional bookbinding practices.
The session will focus on creating a handmade book using the Coptic stitch binding technique, a historical and visually striking method known for its exposed spine and ability to lay completely flat when open. Each participant will complete their own book by the end of the session, gaining practical experience in folding, punching, stitching, and assembling the components.
This workshop is ideal for beginners as well as creative enthusiasts interested in crafts, journaling, or book arts.
All materials are included.
Age: 16+

name: History Beyond Textbooks: How ancient Indians engineered water! - A Fun and Interactive Exploration of India’s Ingenious Water Wisdom
description:
How did ancient Indians store water for an entire year using only seasonal rains? How did they build a city in a place with little water?
Step into an exciting journey to the past to explore how ancient Indians managed water and mastered the seas!
We are inviting curious young minds (ages 11-14) to explore the genius of Indian engineering—from how we saved every drop of rain to how we sailed the high seas to trade with the world. This is not a lesson but a journey from Harappan reservoirs to our very own lakes and keres in Bengaluru!
 
Workshop Details:
📅 Date: 2 May 2026, Saturday
🕒 Time: 3:00 PM – 6:00 PM
📍 Venue: Atta Galatta, Bengaluru
🎟️ Registration Fee: ₹650/-
✨ Limited slots available!
Organisers: The Foundation for Indian Historical and Cultural Research (FIHCR) is a private, non-profit organization that aims to revive, preserve, and promote India’s rich historical and cultural heritage by re-establishing India’s historical and cultural narrative.
As part of this vision, the YUVA Programme by FIHCR is dedicated to making history engaging and relevant for younger audiences. Through illustrated books, national competitions, and interactive workshops, YUVA encourages curiosity, critical thinking, and a deeper connection with India’s past among students and educators.
Venue Partner: Atta Galatta - a vibrant cultural space for books, ideas, and community engagement.
Age: 11-14 yrs

name: The Bougainvillea Circle - Connection Circle
description:
"Sawubona" a Zulu Greeting meaning, "We See You". At Bougainvillea Circles you are offered a space to be heard, seen, welcomed as you are. A non-judgmental, safe, trusted space where you are free to be all that you want to be. Nothing More, Nothing less. No Restrictions, no judgements, just an intimate group of people looking for a space to be real. A space where we`re all less alone for a little while. Where we are seen, how we want to be seen. Conversations, Connections, Community of Compassion. All that makes the world go round, after Love of course!
Remember - Sawubona: We See You.
Age: 18+

name: Queer Films & Feels - Queer Cinema Is Resistance In The Form Of Art
description:
Queer cinema is resistance in the form of art ✊🏳️‍🌈🏳️‍⚧️
In our efforts to feel our feelings against the Trans Act (#againsttransbill), join us for a therapeutic exploration of our gender and sexuality journey through films 🍿📽️🎬
3 indie films in 1 wholesome space 🏡😌
🗓️: 2nd May (Saturday)
⏰: 5 to 7 pm
📍: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
🎫: ₹150 (to cover venue cost & to contribute to mutual aid for trans people)

“City of Mirage”, “Pehli Date” and “Housewife” are all queer indie films. Let’s come together to witness the work of Anjani Chadha (@anjanichadha), Niv (@nivtheyboi), Sulagna Chatterjee (@sulashouldbewriting), and Vai (@vaiyoudothis)
Let’s witness, reflect, and feel 💕
Age: 18+

name: Lino Cut Printing - Workshop
description:
Step into the world of printmaking with a hands-on linocut printing workshop by The Lino Table! This beginner-friendly session invites you to slow down, carve, ink, and print your own artwork from scratch.
This edition of the workshop is themed around cafés and food, drawing inspiration from cozy corners, comforting meals, and everyday indulgences. Whether it’s your favourite cup of coffee, a flaky croissant, or a bustling café scene; translate it into a beautiful handmade print. 
The workshop will be conducted by Chaitra Kachare, Founder and Artist, The Lino Table, who will guide you through the entire process: from understanding the basics of linocut, to carving your own design, and finally printing your artwork to take home.
No prior experience is needed, just bring your curiosity and love for creating.

Age: 16+

name: Healing Wounds In Parental Relationships - An Experential Workshop
description:
Parents are among the most influential figures we encounter in our lifetime. They leave a profound emotional impact on children, shaping everything from the drive to succeed to the choice of romantic partners. The parent–child bond holds immense potential: it can help a child achieve a well‑balanced life, inspire aspirations, and foster fulfilling relationships.
Disruptions in this bond, however, can have debilitating effects. Fear of failure may prevent children from realizing their potential. Fear of rejection can normalize loneliness. Fear of abandonment may lead one to abandon oneself in favor of others. Even successful individuals may live in a state of omnipotent dissatisfaction and discontent with themselves.
This workshop is designed to help participants witness and begin the process of recovering their sense of self— the greatest legacy parents pass on to their children. Together, we will explore ways to cultivate contentment and move toward a more satisfying, authentic life.
Lunch and snacks will be provided are inclusive of the ticket. 
Age: 18+

name: DOSE OF POSITIVE PSYCHOLOGY  - Meetup
description:
In an attempt to bridge a bit of Neuroscience with Positive Psychology, this workshop offers a moment to reflect on our typically fast-paced lives. What small changes can we make to have an overall impact on our well-being? It's something we might already do and perhaps knowing the science behind it can help in making more changes.
Come join us for an interactive session filled with science, poetry and art activities and get your daily dose of Positive Psychology.
Age: 16+

name: Puppet Show - Live Gig
description:
Get ready for a fun-filled, interactive puppet experience for your little ones! 🌸
Join us at Atta galatta, Bangalore for a delightful storytelling session where the classic tales comes alive—
but with a fresh twist ✨
🎭 Experience a unique, indigenous fox puppet crafted especially for children
💡 Discover a new ending and a gentle, meaningful takeaway that goes beyond the usual moral
 
Designed especially for children aged 2 above, this session is:
✨ Interactive
✨ Engaging
✨ Filled with laughter, actions, and participation
A perfect blend of storytelling, puppetry, and imagination—where children don’t just watch the story, they become a part of it 💛
📍 Venue: Attagallata, Bangalore
👶 Age Group: 2+ years
🎭 Format: Live Puppet Show 
Parents can accompany their child.

name: Science Café - Free Science Conversations
description:
Welcome to the second edition of Science Cafe - free science conversations space at Attagalatta on 3rd May from 4 pm to 6 pm. 
This time we will be diving deeper into 'Science in Popular Imagination' based upon science in news/social media/ magazines, sci-fi books and movies adaptations (riding on the wave of Artemis II mission and the movie Project Hail Mary). It is an inclusive safe space for all science, stem enthusiasts and all curious folks. It is time to bring science to the coffee table conversations (inspired by the idea of Cafe Scientifique by Dr. Oliver Sacks)
Age: All

name: Mantras of the Moon - Book Launch & Poetry Reading
description:
Atta Galatta invites you to an evening of poetry, conversation, and reflection with poet Srijani Mitra, celebrating the launch of her new poetry collection Mantras of the Moon.
Published by Red Rook Press, Mantras of the Moon is a lyrical exploration of identity, cultural inheritance, memory, and selfhood. Through meditative and evocative poems, the collection navigates questions of belonging, Indianness, spirituality, and the inner landscapes that shape who we become.
In this special launch event, Srijani Mitra will read from the collection, discuss the ideas and inspirations behind the book, and engage in conversation with the audience about poetry, culture, and the creative process.
About the Author:
Srijani Mitra is a poet and writer whose work explores the intersections of culture, selfhood, and language. She has presented her work in academic and literary spaces including a talk organized by Indian Institute of Technology Dharwad. Mantras of the Moon marks an important addition to contemporary poetry engaging with cultural identity.
Join us for an intimate literary evening celebrating poetry and the power of voice.
Age: 16 and above

name: The Pranic Advantage. Racing Towards The Greatest Career Possibilities, Success & Impact. - Pranic Healing Session
description:
Pranic Healing is a revolutionary no-touch, no-drug tested energy-based treatment that uses Prana, Chi or Energy to improve a wide range of physical, psychological, relationships and financial issues.
Learn About The Power Of Pranic Self-healing for Professionals & Businesses. Get to know how simply and properly you can, Eliminate Constant Stress, Anxiety, Fear, Confusion, Insecurities, Overload & Burnout. Unlock Greater Intelligence, Influence and Productivity.
Achieve Personal & Professional (All-Round) Stability, Success & Fulfillment. Questions & Answers.
Date & Time: 8th May, Friday. 6-7PM IST
Venue: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar, Bangalore
Speaker: Shravya Gubbi (Pranic Healer, 2X Entrepreneur)
Organiser: Pranic Healing for Free & Abundant Living
Age: 16+

name: Rise in Love - An Intimate Book Preview Evening
description:
What is this evening all about?
"A lot of what we've written—and what we'll share—didn't come from having answers. It came from moments where life, as we knew it, wasn't working anymore..."
This isn't a polished book launch.
It's an intimate preview of our upcoming work—born from personal unraveling, relationship shifts, and patterns we all recognize but rarely name.
 
For those who:
● Feel relationships cycling through familiar pain
● Carry questions about identity, love, healing
● Recognize their own patterns in ours
Age: 25+

name: Atta Galatta presents “The BWW Short Story Community” - Literary Meetup
description:
The BWW Short Fiction Community at Atta Galatta will discuss

Three Niles
By Zak Salih
Set during a brief visit to Sudan, Three Niles follows a thirteen-year-old boy traveling with his father to reconnect with family and heritage. As he navigates unfamiliar customs, language barriers, and the expectations of tradition, the story tenderly explores questions of belonging, identity, and the complicated bonds between fathers and sons. Against the backdrop of the meeting of the Blue and White Nile rivers, the narrative reflects on how cultures, generations, and selves converge—sometimes quietly, sometimes with deep emotional force.
Read the text and come for the event.

name: Unspoken: Open Mic Night - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This is not just an open mic.
This is a space to be seen, heard, and felt.
A community gathering where poets, musicians, storytellers, and artists come together—not to perform perfectly, but to express authentically.
Because your voice is not just sound…
It carries your truth.
And speaking your truth requires something deeper—
✨ Clarity
✨ Courage
✨ Emotional release
✨ Inner alignment
 
This evening is designed as a healing experience, where expression becomes a form of transformation.
 
Through guided awareness and collective energy, we will gently open the channels within—
your mind, your body, your emotions, your soul—
so that what you share comes from a place of truth, not fear.
 
🌿 This is a space where:
 
You don’t have to impress, you just have to be real
You don’t perform, you release
You don’t seek validation, you reconnect with yourself
As a community, we will:
✨ Heal together
✨ Express freely
✨ Rise collectively
Because when individuals find their voice,
✨ the entire community transforms.

Age: 18+

name: The Listening Room - A Connection Circle
description:
20 people. 1 quiet room. No phones. A circle where people share the songs and the stories that stayed with them. 🫶
We’re bringing you an intimate, deeply human, musical experience with real people, real stories, and real memories. 💌
Bring that one song that arrived like a whisper, a serendipity, the kind that lingered long after the last note. That’s the magic we’re after.
Come as you are. Bring a song, or just your presence. The rest will find you. All Materials Included.
About Third Place:
Where dinners spark real connections, experiences bring us closer, and evenings unfold beautifully, unscripted. Just say “yes” and discover the people, places, and moments you were never supposed to miss. Reserve your seat now & see where the night takes you.
Age: 18+

name: THE ROAR AND THE SQUEAK! - Puppet Show, Art Session And Some Fun-Time
description:
The mighty lion and the tiny mouse return in a charming puppet show filled with sing-along songs and storytelling through handmade puppets. Children will then be guided through an art activity to create an animated lion – the imaginative storytelling version of the character on paper!
A perfect blend of art, imagination, and storytelling: come and create your own jungle tale!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.
Art. Story. Repeat!

name: Date With My Mother - Mother's day Special Edition Experience
description:
Step away from the rush of daily life and gift yourself something truly priceless — quality time with your mother.
Join us for a heartwarming evening filled with love, laughter, meaningful conversations, and beautiful memories. Whether you are a son or daughter, young adult or grown-up, this is your chance to celebrate the woman who has always been there for you.
✨ What to Expect:
• Fun bonding activities
• Create a Gratitude jar ( heartfelt moments)
• Interactive games - what is my fav , and more 
• Memory-making experiences
• Special surprises
• Warm refreshments
🌸 Because no matter how old we grow, we are always our mother’s child.
📅 Date: 10th May
⏰ Time: 6:00 PM to 7:30 PM
📍 Venue: Atta Gatta, Indira nagar 
💰 Investment: ₹1999 ( for Mother & Child Pair) 
💝 Come create memories that will stay in your heart forever.
 
Limited seats available – Book your spot now!

Age: 18+

name: Curious Kids’ Creative Workshop - 5 Day Summer Camp
description:
Let your child imagine, explore, 
and create, one playful day at a time. 
No step-by-step instructions, no right or wrong. 
This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

description:

and create, one playful day at a time.

This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

name: AG Lit Sisters: The Divakaruni Diaries - Thematic Book Club
description:
This month’s pick feels a little special 💛
We are reading Chitra Banerjee Divakaruni and honestly, her stories have a way of making you pause and think, “wait, why was her voice never heard like this before?”
It is mythology, but softer. More human. More real.
Come hang out with us, talk books, share your take, or just listen and soak it all in. No pressure to be “well-read”, just show up.
 
16 May 1:30 PM Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
Free entry. Just bring yourself.
Question of the day Which character stayed with you long after you finished the book
Keywords books, honest, women, stories, real, feelings, conversations, comfort, community, reading
Age: 18+

name: Alfaz-e-Andaaz - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This event is a blend of Hindi, Urdu poetry and literature,
where you all are heartily welcomed into the wonderful world of words.
Date & Time: May 16, 2026, from 4:30 PM to 5:30 PM
Free Entry
Location: Atta Galatta, 1st Stage Indiranagar, Bangalore
Organizer: Shelly Aggarwal (Hindi Author)

Age: 16+

name: Curious Kids’ Creative Workshop - 5 Day Summer Camp
description:
Let your child imagine, explore, 
and create, one playful day at a time. 
No step-by-step instructions, no right or wrong. 
This workshop gives children the space to explore materials freely, make their own choices, and express what they’re thinking and feeling. A hands-on, sensory-rich experience that builds creativity, confidence, and curiosity
Age: 2-5 yrs old

name: Why Did You Do It Like That? - A Dialogue with the Authors of And How Do You Feel About That?
description:
And How Do You Feel About That? is a book that brings together fifty stories based on real clinical work. 
It was written with the intention of demystifying psychotherapy—offering readers a glimpse of what actually happens in the conversation between therapist and client. It is written in a simple, conversational style that the layperson can relate with, but has a richness and depth that those in the world of therapy will uniquely appreciate.
Through these vignettes, the book also showcases the range and depth of Transactional Analysis psychotherapy and how its ideas come alive in real clinical encounters.
In this two-hour session, the authors, Aruna Gopakumar and Yashodhara Lal,  invite therapists, counsellors, coaches, and other helping professionals into a conversation about the thinking behind these stories. Participants who have read the book are especially welcome to bring their curiosity and questions: Why did you intervene in that moment? Tell us about your thinking here or tell us more about this technique. 
During the session, they will read short sections from selected stories and pause to unpack the therapeutic decisions within them. The authors will reflect on their learning, demonstrate selected methods, and discuss what it means to work relationally with clients.
Rather than a lecture, the evening is intended as a dialogue among practitioners interested in the craft of therapy. It is also an opportunity to meet and connect with other therapists on a relaxed Sunday evening.
Practitioners of all modalities, all experience levels welcome. Participants who have read the book will likely find the discussion particularly enriching.
Age: 18+

name: The Darker Moon - Book Reading and Signing
description:
Inviting everyone to a book reading by Syamanthaka, where she will read a few pages from her debut fiction novel – The Darker New Moon.
An IT professional, exploring her creative side, she would answer a few audience questions on how she self-published the book.
She would also be super happy to sign your copy of The Darker New Moon.
---
This event is open to all ages, although the genre itself is more suitable for “grown ups” who love reading a good mystery.

name: A Love Story of 12.5 Lacs - Solo Storytelling Act
description:
A software engineer chasing his acting dreams falls in love with a young aspiring actress. As they navigate auditions and struggles, their bond deepens—until one unexpected incident changes everything.
Without offering a real reason, she asks to break up, leaving him confused, heartbroken, and searching for answers.
Years later, during the COVID pandemic, a single message brings the past rushing back—with just one word: “Sorry.”
A poignant tale of love, silence, and unanswered questions that never truly fade.
Written and performed by Vaibhav Mishra, this solo act blends reality with fiction—promising moments that will feel deeply personal and relatable to everyone.
Age: 16+

name: Hired. Fired. Tired. - Book Launch
description:
Come join us for an afternoon of fun, banter, empowerment, and honest conversations on womanhood, workplace nightmares, women’s leadership, cognitive biases-and much more.
On May 24, join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Hired. Fired. Tired. by debut author Tushara Reddy- a candid and thought-provoking book exploring the realities of the modern tech workplace. Framed through the perspective of a first-generation millennial professional, the book unpacks the often-unspoken politics of the industry-where ambition, identity, and survival frequently collide.
Told from a woman’s point of view, Hired. Fired. Tired. speaks to those navigating toxic work cultures, imposter syndrome, layoffs, and the quiet pressures of being a newcomer. At once deeply personal and broadly relatable, the book gives voice to the silent struggles of everyday work life while imagining bold possibilities for a more humane and equitable tech future. Tushara also invites readers to be part of her personal journey of writing toward a more utopian tech world-one where empathy, fairness, and opportunity shape the future of work.
As a bonus, the evening will also feature a panel discussion with global tech leaders, exploring real stories, challenges, and ideas for building healthier workplaces in the tech industry.
Tushara Reddy is a mid-senior product manager with over nine years of experience across India, the UK, and the USA. An alumna of Queen Mary University of London, she currently lives in Bengaluru, where she balances work, workouts, and writing in her spare time.

Age: 18 and above

name: Meera and the Magic Kolam - Book Launch & Art Workshop
description:
Step into a playful, creative experience inspired by Meera and the Magic Kolam—a story that celebrates imagination, tradition, and self-expression.
This 2-hour interactive session introduces participants to the beauty of kolam, a traditional South Indian art form created using patterns of dots and lines, symbolizing harmony, rhythm, and everyday creativity.
The session includes a live storytelling and reading of the book, followed by a hands-on workshop where participants create their own unique kolam designs on canvas.
Each participant will also receive a copy of Meera and the Magic Kolam to take home.
Open to kids aged 5 and above, this workshop blends storytelling, art, and mindful creation—no prior experience needed.
